prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Verily, in the third moon of the quarter, the number of "active users" did outpace the village goats thrice over, though none could show me these users in the castle courtyard. The scribes, clad in denim pantaloons, spake oft of "cloud ARR," which I assumed to be an angry sky spirit until a wizard named Mark revealed it was money one cannot hold. Great concern was given to something called "churn," yet none dared stir the dairy, instead bewailing customers vanishing into the ether like spirits at cockcrow. By Saint Isidore, I recorded with trembling quill that all was up and to the right, though precisely what had ascended—and wherefore—I leave to God and the auditors.
